items.q: Why does the colour of a transition metal complex change when ligands are exchanged?
items.correct: Different ligands cause different amounts of d-orbital splitting, so different wavelengths of light are absorbed.
items.misconception: The colour changes because the metal ion itself changes oxidation state.
items.random_wrong: The colour changes because the new ligand is itself coloured.
items.why: Students assume colour comes from the ligand or from a redox change; they miss that the same metal ion gives different colours depending on which ligands are bound.

items.q: What is the purpose of a salt bridge in an electrochemical cell?
items.correct: It completes the circuit by allowing ions to flow between the half-cells, maintaining electrical neutrality.
items.misconception: The salt bridge carries electrons between the two electrodes.
items.random_wrong: The salt bridge prevents the solutions from reacting with each other.
items.why: Students confuse the internal circuit (ion flow through salt bridge) with the external circuit (electron flow through wire) — both are needed, but they carry different charge carriers.

items.q: Why does atomic radius increase down a group?
items.correct: Each successive element has an additional electron shell, increasing the distance of outer electrons from the nucleus.
items.misconception: Atoms get bigger down a group because they have more protons attracting more matter.
items.random_wrong: Atoms get bigger down a group because nuclei swell when they have more neutrons.
items.why: Students attribute size to mass/protons (a 'more stuff = bigger' intuition) rather than to the number of shells.
The answer examiners want is: Bloch and Purcell 1946. “Watson and Crick 1953” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Typical O-H stretch in IR appears at:
items.why: It's 3200-3600 cm-1. “1700 cm-1” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Year Bloch and Purcell first demonstrated NMR?
items.why: The real figure is 1946 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
